name: Screenshot MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for capturing screenshots from the local desktop or specific application windows. Enables AI agents with vision capabilities to see the current state of the screen, capture specific regions, and use visual context for UI automation, testing, and vision-based workflows.
use_cases: Capturing desktop screenshots for AI vision analysis and UI understanding
Monitoring application state visually from AI testing and QA agents
Providing screen context for AI agents assisting with UI navigation tasks
Visual debugging and documentation capture via AI-driven screenshot automation
not_for: Web-only screenshot capture (use browser automation MCP instead)
Video recording or continuous screen monitoring
best_when: A developer using AI coding assistants or automation agents wants to give AI agents visual context about the current state of the desktop UI for vision-based task assistance.
avoid_when: You're running in headless/server environments. Also: requires display access — not suitable for remote server automation without X/VNC.

auth.notes: No authentication — local desktop access. OS-level screen recording permissions may be required.

pricing.notes: Free open source. Requires OS screen recording permission on macOS.

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Screenshots capture everything on screen — may include sensitive information visible at time of capture
macOS requires Screen Recording permission granted in System Preferences — setup step required
Multi-monitor setups may require specifying which display to capture
Screenshot data must be passed to a vision-capable model — not all AI models support image input
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Captures full screen including sensitive visible content. OS screen recording permissions required. Screenshots may contain passwords, private data visible on screen.

security_score_components.security_notes: Privacy risk — captures everything visible on screen. Grant screen recording only to trusted agents. Local-only.
